data.title: ADP Weekly Employment Report: Private sector employers added an average of 11,500 jobs per week in the four weeks ending December 6.
data.description: According to data disclosed in ADP's weekly employment report, in the four weeks ending December 6, 2025, private sector employers added an average of 11,500 jobs per week. (Jinshi)

data.title: SharpLink received 460 ETH in staking rewards last week, bringing its total staking rewards to 9701 ETH.
data.description: Ethereum treasury company SharpLink released data showing that it received 460 ETH in staking rewards last week, bringing its total staking rewards to 9,701 ETH since the launch of its staking strategy.

data.title: Spot silver rose more than 2% on the day, currently trading at $70.40 per ounce.
data.description: Market data shows that spot silver rose more than 2.00% intraday, currently trading at $70.40 per ounce. New York silver futures extended their gains to 3.00% intraday, currently trading at $70.63 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: BC Card Completes Stablecoin Payment Pilot for Foreign Users
data.description: According to Odaily, South Korean credit card company BC Card has successfully completed a pilot project for stablecoin payments. This initiative enables foreign users to pay local merchants using stablecoins. Users can convert stablecoins stored in overseas wallets partnered with BC Card into digital prepaid cards.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Advocates Major Financial Merger
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has announced efforts to facilitate a significant merger between Main Street and Wall Street. Amid a 6% decline in Bitcoin prices in 2025, the Trump administration has introduced the 'Beautiful Act,' which provides newborns with a $1,000 'Trump Account' to encourage investment awareness among the public. Meanwhile, Wall Street financial giants like BlackRock have accumulated nearly $70 billion in Bitcoin ETFs, indicating the rapid integration of cryptocurrencies into mainstream finance. Bessent forecasts that tax reductions will lead to a 'major tax refund year' in the first quarter of 2026. Additionally, the Trump administration plans to introduce a market structure bill in early 2026 to further regulate the stablecoin market.

data.title: USD/CHF Reaches Three-Month Low Amid Continued Decline
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the USD/CHF currency pair has continued its downward trend, reaching a three-month low. The latest figures show a decline of 0.57%, with the exchange rate now at 0.7873.

data.title: U.S. Unemployment Rate Reaches Four-Year High Amid Trump's Controversial Claims
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. unemployment rate hit a four-year high of 4.6% in November. U.S. President Donald Trump controversially claimed that a single phone call could bring the unemployment rate to zero. Analysts warn that such dismissive remarks could pose significant risks by undermining the credibility of official data, which is crucial for businesses making informed hiring decisions. Trump's assertion that the U.S. job market is better than reported may lead to doubts about his credibility, especially concerning the economy, a vulnerable issue for him in the midterm elections.

data.title: Binance Alpha's Audiera (BEAT) Airdrop Rewards Now Live
data.description: Binance announced on X that Binance Alpha's second wave of Audiera (BEAT) airdrop rewards are now live. Users with at least 225 Binance Alpha Points can claim an airdrop of 9 BEAT tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the reward pool is not fully distributed, the score threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes.Claiming the airdrop will consume 15 Binance Alpha Points. Users must confirm their claim on the Alpha Events page within 24 hours; otherwise, it will be deemed that users have given up claiming the airdrop.

data.title: Binance Alpha is conducting its second round of Audiera (BEAT) airdrops, with a minimum of 225 points required.
data.description: According to official sources, Binance Alpha is conducting its second round of Audiera (BEAT) airdrops. Users with at least 225 Alpha Points can claim 9 BEAT tokens on a first-come, first-served basis. If the reward pool is not fully distributed, the points threshold will automatically decrease by 5 points every 5 minutes. Claiming the airdrop will cost 15 Alpha Points.

data.title: U.S. Economic Growth Data May Impact Dollar Strength
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, XTB analyst Hani Abuagla has highlighted concerns regarding the U.S. economy's third-quarter growth data. If the figures fall short of expectations, the dollar could face significant pressure. Signs of economic cooling might bolster expectations for further interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve next year, potentially lowering yields and weakening the dollar further. The reduction in year-end liquidity and shifts in global monetary policy could intensify this sensitivity, particularly with the recent interest rate hike by the Bank of Japan possibly attracting capital inflows into the yen.

data.title: A whale deposited 195 WBTC, worth $17.05 million, into Binance four hours ago.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale who spent $38.16 million to build a position in WBTC over the past month is reducing its holdings. Four hours ago, it deposited 195 WBTC (approximately $17.05 million) into Binance. Including the portion deposited three weeks ago, it appears to have sold 245 WBTC, which would have yielded a profit of $325,000 if sold. The remaining 200 WBTC still have a floating profit of $389,000, during which time the price of the coin once rose to a high of $94,555.

data.title: Ethereum Contract Size Limitations Discussed Amid Security Concerns
data.description: According to Odaily, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin addressed community concerns regarding the contract size limit on the Ethereum network. He explained that the restriction is in place due to considerations related to Denial-of-Service (DoS) risks. Buterin noted that once Ethereum's state structure is upgraded to a unified binary tree (EIP-7864), it may be possible to remove the contract size limit. However, challenges remain in terms of gas fees and mechanism design for deploying large contracts. Currently, the cost per byte for contract creation sets the practical limit at approximately 82KB.

data.title: The U.S. Department of Justice released 30,000 pages of documents related to Epstein, clarifying that the allegations against Trump were false.
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced on the evening of December 23 that it had officially released nearly 30,000 pages of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein. The Department of Justice specifically pointed out that these documents contained false accusations against President Trump that were submitted to the FBI before the 2020 election, and explicitly stated that these accusations were "baseless and false." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Significant SOL Transfer from Wintermute to Anonymous Address
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Arkham data reveals that at 20:35, a total of 17,600 SOL was transferred from Wintermute to an anonymous address starting with HtkxvvC14s. The transfer is notable due to the substantial amount involved.

data.title: Analysts: Any signs of an economic slowdown could strengthen expectations of further interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve next year.
data.description: XTB analyst Hani Abuagla stated in a report that the dollar will be very vulnerable if US third-quarter economic growth data falls short of expectations. Any signs of an economic slowdown could strengthen expectations of further interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve next year, thereby lowering yields and further weakening the dollar. Reduced year-end liquidity and recent changes in global monetary policy could exacerbate this sensitivity. In particular, the Bank of Japan's recent rate hike may encourage capital inflows into the yen, which would further pressure the dollar if US economic data disappoints. (Jinshi)

data.title: Why Are Huntington Ingalls Shares Jumping? Navy Contract Drives Gains
data.description:
Huntington Ingalls stock moved higher in premarket trading following news that the U. S. Navy awarded the company a contract for a new warship program.
source: https://tokenist.com/why-are-huntington-ingalls-shares-jumping-navy-contract-drives-gains/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=why-are-huntington-ingalls-shares-jumping-navy-contract-drives-gains

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Releases Epstein-Related Documents
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Department of Justice has announced the release of nearly 30,000 pages of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein. These documents include unfounded and sensational allegations against U.S. President Donald Trump, which were submitted to the FBI before the 2020 election.

The Justice Department stated that these allegations are baseless and fabricated, asserting that if they had any credibility, they would have been used against President Trump. However, in line with its commitment to legal transparency, the department will release these documents while ensuring the protection of Epstein's victims.

The Justice Department began releasing investigation documents related to the Epstein case on December 19, following the requirements of the Epstein Files Transparency Act. This act, passed by Congress in November and signed by President Trump, mandates the disclosure of all non-confidential records related to the investigation, prosecution, and incarceration of Epstein by December 19.

data.title: Vitalik responded to the fact that Ethereum still has a contract size cap: due to DoS risk considerations, it may be lifted after EIP-7864.
data.description: In response to the community discussion about Ethereum still having a contract size cap, Vitalik Buterin stated that this is due to DoS risk considerations. He explained that once Ethereum's state structure is upgraded to a unified binary tree (EIP-7864), it is expected to achieve unlimited contract size, but the gas fees and mechanism design for publishing extremely large contracts still need to be addressed. Currently, based on the cost per byte of creation, the actual contract size cap is approximately 82KB.

data.title: Azuki launches Telegram game "Azuki Alley Escape"
data.description: Animoca Brands announced a collaboration between its Web3 gaming platform GAMEE and Azuki to release the Telegram skateboarding parkour game "Azuki Alley Escape." Players can avoid obstacles, collect rewards, and compete on leaderboards. The game features Azuki's art style and storyline.

data.title: Trump claimed that the US job market was better than it actually was and that the unemployment rate could be reduced to zero with just "one phone call".
data.description: The US unemployment rate hit a four-year high of 4.6% in November, yet Trump argued that he could bring it to zero with just "a phone call." Analysts point out that this blatant disregard for public perception is putting him at great risk. Questioning the validity of official data could undermine the government's crucial role in helping businesses make informed hiring decisions. Trump's claim that the US job market is better than it actually is could raise doubts about his credibility, especially on the economy, his most vulnerable issue in the midterm elections. (Jinshi)
